we need the trust level in TrustLevelManager which isn't found in cmHandleStatePerCmHandle
I don't think that is necessarily the case, the dmi trust level could be NONE and the cm handle trust level could be NONE so no notification would be sent

Hi Toine, the reason why we have an extra method parameter at this point is for initial registration use-case. 

Via initial registration use-case, customer can report initial trust level of cm-handles. In order to process that incoming trust level, we thought we must pass these (request parameters) up to this point of the code so that we can process the notifications (based-on-req-parameters) upon a successful cm-handle registration completion.

Hi Toine, the parameter name can be anything to handle the code at this point. 

The reason why we decide on 'candidate' is that we only need to process notification for the cm-handle ids that belong to the dmi which has different health status than previous health status (within the time frame in scheduled job).

We are open to have a good variable naming if you can suggest. Thanks.
create a map for TrustLevel instead: Map<String, TrustLevel>

instead of a map we just need the KEYS ALL of them can be set to the INITIAL state: ADVISED

would prefer to be a bit more explicit here (aslo make sure to use the assert keyword!) e.g

def actualAvcs = ...
def expectedAvc = ...
assert actualAvcs == [expectedAvc]

OR

def actualAvcs = ...
def expectedAvc = ...
assert actualAvs.size() == 1
def actualAvc == actualAvcs[0]
assert actualAvc == expectedAvc
